behavior: Often inconspicuous in low, shrubby cover; males give a rich warbling song from exposed perches. Forages on the ground and in low vegetation, and commonly flicks or twitches its tail sideways.
field_marks: Stocky, full-bodied songbird with a very large triangular bill.
Adult male is deep blue overall, often with a small black mask before the eye.
Bill is bicolored: dark upper mandible and silvery lower mandible.
Two conspicuous wingbars, with a chestnut upper bar and paler lower bar.
Dark, rounded wings and a fairly long dark tail; tail may be flicked sideways.
Females and immature birds are warm cinnamon-brown, with wingbars and often bluish tail or scattered blue feathers.
habitat: Semi-open, shrubby vegetation: brushy fields, hedgerows, woodland edges, young regrowth, thickets, and open weedy ground; also uses shrubby growth near water in drier landscapes.

sources.title: Blue Grosbeak Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
